segments.text: UPSC just changed the rules for civil
services. Subscribe full disclosure and
I'll quickly break down what actually
changed in this notification. 2026
notification introduces strict
restrictions on who can reappear for the
exam after getting selected. First, if
an aspirant is already serving in IAS or
IFS, she is not allowed to appear in CSE
2026. Practically to compete again in
future, she has to resign and still
satisfy age and attempt limits. Second,
for IPS officers, they can still write
the exam again, but from cse 2026
onward, IPS is locked and the selected
person can't opt for or be allotted IPS
again. Third, for group A service
allotties, if they get IPS or another
group A service via CSE 2026, they'll
get one special chance to reappear in
cse 2027 with a one-time exemption from
training. But from cse 2028 onwards, no
more attempts unless you resign from
your allocated service. Are these rules
necessary to stop candidates from
endlessly reattempting while blocking
new aspirants? What are your thoughts on
this? Comment below.
